steps.dialogue: Great job! Let's review what we learned this lesson.

We learned that equivalent fractions don't just look the same in bars—they land at the exact same position on a number line. When we placed equivalent fractions at the same point—like [fraction numerator=1 denominator=2]one half[/fraction] and [fraction numerator=2 denominator=4]two fourths[/fraction]—we proved they're equal.

We also demonstrated that some fractions equal whole numbers—even whole numbers greater than 1. [fraction numerator=4 denominator=4]Four fourths[/fraction] landed exactly at 1, and then [fraction numerator=8 denominator=4]eight fourths[/fraction] reached 2!
The problems we worked through today were our most advanced yet. The patterns you've recognized and the skills you've built from past lessons helped prepare you to solve them.
